raw: {"weights":{"R1.1":0.7,"R1.2":0.7,"R1.3":0.7,"R1.4":0.7,"R1.5":0.3,"R1.6":0.3,"R1.7":0.3,"RX.1":1.0,"RX.2":1.0,"RX.3":1.0,"RX.4":1.0,"RX.5":1.0},"active_set":["R1.1","R1.2","R1.3","R1.4","RX.1","RX.2","RX.3","RX.4","RX.5"],"rationale":{"R1.1":"\"What do you think?\" applied to a research paper most naturally demands evaluation of the core thesis — whether the contribution claim (first compositional PPL semantics for role-induced agent stochasticity) is coherent and well-motivated.","R1.2":"Any substantive opinion requires checking whether the denotational account, the importance-sampling instantiation, and the three emergent properties form a logically consistent chain.","R1.3":"The paper asserts wall-clock parity and calibrated posterior estimates on three benchmarks; a general opinion must assess whether those experimental claims credibly support the broader PPL-for-agents pitch.","R1.4":"An explicit \"first\" claim is made twice; assessing the actual delta vs. prior PPL work and prior multi-agent LLM frameworks is load-bearing for any meaningful evaluation of the paper."},"clarification_needed":true,"clarification_question":"Is your primary interest in evaluating the conceptual soundness and novelty of the \"first PPL semantics\" claim (favoring R1.1/R1.4 as load-bearing), or in assessing whether the reported benchmark evidence credibly supports the efficiency and calibration claims (favoring R1.3 as load-bearing)?"}

rationale.R1.1: "What do you think?" applied to a research paper most naturally demands evaluation of the core thesis — whether the contribution claim (first compositional PPL semantics for role-induced agent stochasticity) is coherent and well-motivated.
rationale.R1.2: Any substantive opinion requires checking whether the denotational account, the importance-sampling instantiation, and the three emergent properties form a logically consistent chain.
rationale.R1.3: The paper asserts wall-clock parity and calibrated posterior estimates on three benchmarks; a general opinion must assess whether those experimental claims credibly support the broader PPL-for-agents pitch.
rationale.R1.4: An explicit "first" claim is made twice; assessing the actual delta vs. prior PPL work and prior multi-agent LLM frameworks is load-bearing for any meaningful evaluation of the paper.

parsed.clarification_question: Is your primary interest in evaluating the conceptual soundness and novelty of the "first PPL semantics" claim (favoring R1.1/R1.4 as load-bearing), or in assessing whether the reported benchmark evidence credibly supports the efficiency and calibration claims (favoring R1.3 as load-bearing)?
